// Overlapping (|->): Consequent checked in SAME cycle
// Non-overlapping (|=>): Consequent checked in NEXT cycle
// Consecutive [*n]: Signal holds for n cycles continuously
// Goto [->n]: Signal occurs n times (non-consecutive), implies immediate follow
// 1. Valid must remain stable until Ready
// Forces explicit declaration of all signals (Best Practice)
// Simple immediate assertion with auto-stringified error
// Note: Use %s to print the message argument
// Prints time, module path (%m) and message
// Prints variable name (stringified) and its value
// Requires scope signals: name_en, name_next, clk, rst_n
always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin \\
// Returns a random element from an array
// Checkers can be instantiated inside modules, interfaces, or procedural blocks
// 'let' is like `define but with scope and type safety
// Similar to Java Interface - defines pure virtual methods
// Use 'context' if C code calls exported SV functions
// Use 'pure' for math functions (optimizable, no globals)

body: // sum() returns width of element unless casted
option.weight = ${1:0}; // Set to 0 to exclude from total grade
type_option.merge_instances = ${4:1}; // Merge all instances for total calc
// Combine coverage from all instances into one 'type' bucket
// val occurs n times (non-consecutive), immediately followed by next val
// 1...1...1 => 2 (The transition happens immediately after the last 1)
// val occurs n times (anywhere), eventually followed by next val
// := Operator: Weight applies to EACH item/value
// :/ Operator: Weight is SPLIT across the range
// Count number of bits matching the mask (e.g., '0, '1, 'x, 'z)
// True if exactly one bit is 1
// True if zero or one bit is 1
// True if any bit is X or Z
// Standard way to calculate address width: localparam AW = $clog2(DEPTH);
